entities.name: SPERMOPHAGUS NIGER
entities.description: A bruchid beetle that acts as a major pest of roselle seeds during post-harvest storage.

entities.name: ROSELLE
entities.description: Hibiscus sabdariffa L., a plant whose seeds are infested by S. niger.

entities.name: POST-HARVEST STORAGE
entities.description: The period and environment after harvest where roselle seeds are stored in granaries.

entities.name: BURKINA FASO
entities.description: The country where the study on roselle seed infestation was conducted.

entities.name: TOUGAN
entities.description: A location in Burkina Faso where roselle seed samples were collected.

entities.name: DEDOUGOU
entities.description: A location in Burkina Faso where roselle seed samples were collected.

entities.name: OUAGADOUGOU
entities.description: A location in Burkina Faso where roselle seed samples were collected.

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The broader region where post-harvest management of roselle is relevant.

entities.name: SEED PERFORATION RATE
entities.description: The percentage of seeds with insect emergence holes, ranging from 1.8% to 4%.

entities.name: SEXUAL DIMORPHISM
entities.description: The morphological differences between male and female S. niger, where males are smaller and weigh less.

entities.name: POST-EMBRYONIC DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The life cycle of S. niger within the seed, consisting of four larval instars and one pupal stage.

entities.name: LABORATORY REARING
entities.description: The process of raising insects in a controlled environment to study their morphology and development.

entities.name: POST-HARVEST MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Strategies to manage and protect roselle seeds after harvest from pests.
